item_a.name: Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed
item_a.image_alt: Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed with adjustable air chambers and smart technology
pros: Adjustable firmness from 0-100 on each side
Responsive sleep tracking and biometric monitoring
Zero gravity preset and partner snore technology
Long-term durability with replaceable air chambers
cons: Higher maintenance with air pump components
Can feel less contouring than memory foam
item_a.best_for: Couples with different firmness preferences who want detailed sleep tracking and the ability to fine-tune their mattress over time.

item_b.name: Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid
item_b.image_alt: Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt mattress showing memory foam layers and hybrid construction
pros: Superior pressure relief with proprietary TEMPUR material
Excellent motion isolation for undisturbed sleep
No mechanical parts or electricity required
cons: Fixed firmness level cannot be adjusted
Retains more heat than air-based mattresses
Heavy weight (100-150 lbs) makes moving difficult
item_b.best_for: Individuals seeking exceptional pressure relief and body contouring who have already identified their preferred firmness level.

comparison_summary: Sleep Number gives you adjustable air chambers and sleep tracking tech, while Tempur-Pedic delivers memory foam pressure relief without complexity. Sleep Number adapts to changing needs over time, but Tempur-Pedic costs less upfront and requires zero maintenance.
verdict_text: Sleep Number 360 dominates for couples needing different firmness levels, while Tempur-Pedic crushes it for pressure relief without the tech headaches.

key_differences.item_a: 0-100 adjustable scale on each side via air chambers
key_differences.item_b: Fixed firmness (soft, medium, or firm models available)

key_differences.item_a: Good support but less contouring than foam
key_differences.item_b: Exceptional contouring with NASA-developed TEMPUR material

key_differences.item_a: Built-in SleepIQ technology with heart rate and breathing monitoring
Moderate - air can transfer some movement
key_differences.item_b: Excellent - memory foam absorbs nearly all motion

key_differences.item_a: Cooler sleeping with air circulation and DualTemp layer option
key_differences.item_b: Retains more heat, though newer models include cooling gel

key_differences.item_a: Requires electricity, potential air pump servicing

seo_content:
I've tested both Sleep Number and Tempur-Pedic extensively, and they represent completely opposite philosophies in mattress design.

Sleep Number built their entire brand around adjustable air chambers. You can dial in firmness from 0 to 100, which sounds gimmicky until you actually use it. The Sleep Number 360 i8 is their flagship model, and it's genuinely impressive for couples. Each side adjusts independently—your partner can sleep at a soft 35 while you're comfortable at a firm 75. The SleepIQ technology tracks everything: heart rate variability, breathing rate, movement patterns. All that data flows into a smartphone app.

What surprised me most was the responsive air system that detects snoring and automatically raises the head position. It actually works. The downside? You're looking at $3,999-$5,499, and the whole system needs electricity to function. If your power goes out, you're sleeping on an air mattress.

Tempur-Pedic takes a different approach entirely. The TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid combines their NASA-developed memory foam with a traditional coil system. No apps, no settings to adjust, no pumps humming at night. Just exceptional pressure relief that molds to your body.

I've found Tempur-Pedic superior for pressure points around shoulders, hips, and lower back. Side sleepers particularly benefit from this contouring. Motion isolation beats Sleep Number handily—when your partner moves, you won't feel it. The price range of $2,199-$3,799 offers better entry-level value, though you're locked into whatever firmness you choose. No adjustments later if your preferences change.

Here's where Sleep Number really shines: adaptability over time. Weight fluctuations, injuries, aging—all these change what firmness you need. Sleep Number lets you adjust instead of buying a new mattress. That's powerful.

Tempur-Pedic requires basically zero maintenance. No mechanical parts to break, no motors, no electricity bills. Temperature is another factor I can't ignore. Sleep Number's air chambers allow airflow that keeps you cooler. Tempur-Pedic's dense foam traps heat, even with their cooling gel infusions in newer models.

The decision comes down to this: Do you want adjustability and sleep tracking data, or do you want proven pressure relief without the complexity? Sleep Number if you're tech-inclined and your needs might change. Tempur-Pedic if you know what you like and want something that just works.

faq.q: Which lasts longer, Sleep Number or Tempur-Pedic?
faq.a: Both typically last 10+ years, but Sleep Number has an edge because you can replace the air chambers. I've seen Sleep Numbers go 15+ years, while Tempur-Pedic foam usually maintains good support for 10-12 years before you notice sagging.

faq.q: Is Sleep Number better than Tempur-Pedic for back pain?
faq.a: Tempur-Pedic usually wins for back pain thanks to its contouring properties that relieve pressure points. That said, Sleep Number's adjustability can be just as effective if you dial in your perfect firmness. It really depends on whether you prefer adaptability or targeted pressure relief.

faq.q: Do Sleep Number mattresses need special sheets?
faq.a: Nope, they use standard sheet sizes. If you get an adjustable base though, grab some deep-pocket sheets since the mattress sits higher when you elevate the head or feet.

faq.q: Which is cooler to sleep on, Sleep Number or Tempur-Pedic?
faq.a: Sleep Number sleeps cooler, hands down. Air chambers allow airflow that memory foam just can't match. Tempur-Pedic's dense foam holds onto body heat even with their cooling technologies built in.

faq.q: Can you flip a Tempur-Pedic or Sleep Number mattress?
faq.a: Don't flip either one—they're designed for one-sided use with specific support layers. You should rotate them 180 degrees every 3-6 months to prevent uneven wear, but that's it.

faq.q: Is Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed better than Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid?
faq.a: It depends on your priorities. Sleep Number 360 i8 dominates for couples with different firmness needs and those who want sleep tracking tech, but Tempur-Pedic crushes it for pure pressure relief and simplicity without the maintenance hassle.

faq.q: Should I buy Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed or Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid?
faq.a: Choose Sleep Number 360 i8 if you want customizable firmness and smart sleep features that adapt over time. Pick Tempur-Pedic if you value hassle-free pressure relief, lower upfront cost, and zero tech complications.

faq.q: What is the difference between Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed and Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid?
faq.a: Sleep Number 360 i8 uses adjustable air chambers and sleep tracking technology, while Tempur-Pedic relies on memory foam for passive pressure relief. Sleep Number costs more upfront but adapts to changing needs, while Tempur-Pedic is cheaper and requires no maintenance.

meta_title: Sleep Number vs Tempur-Pedic (2026): Best for Couples vs Back Pain?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Sleep Number vs Tempur-Pedic mattresses. We break down firmness adjustment, pressure relief, sleep tracking, and value to help you pick the right premium mattress.
comparison_intro: When deciding between the Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed vs Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid, it helps to understand what sets these two popular mattresses apart. The key difference between Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed and Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid comes down to how they approach comfort: one leans heavily on customizable technology, while the other focuses on pure material science. Whether you're choosing the Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed or Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid depends on your priorities, so we've broken down the Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed compared to Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid across all the factors that matter most to help you figure out which is better for your sleep style.
